name: Docparser Document Parsing API
what_it_does: Cloud document parsing platform with REST API — extracting structured data from PDFs, Word documents, and scanned images using OCR and rule-based parsing templates, enabling automated data pipelines for invoices, contracts, forms, and reports.
use_cases: Automating invoice data extraction (vendor, amount, line items) from PDF uploads via API
Parsing lease agreements or contracts to extract key terms into structured JSON
Integrating Docparser with Zapier or webhooks for no-code document processing workflows
Extracting form field data from standardized PDF forms for database ingestion
Processing high-volume document batches with webhook notification on completion
not_for: Unstructured document understanding requiring AI/NLP (use AWS Textract or Azure Form Recognizer)
Real-time document processing with sub-second requirements
Handwritten document processing beyond basic OCR capabilities
best_when: A team processes high-volume standardized documents (invoices, purchase orders, receipts, forms) with consistent layouts and needs rule-based extraction without AI complexity.
avoid_when: Documents have highly variable layouts, contain significant handwriting, or require semantic understanding rather than pattern-based field extraction.

auth.notes: API key from Docparser account. Passed as Authorization header (Basic auth with API key as username, empty password) or query parameter. Parser ID required for document routing. No OAuth2 or scopes. Webhook secret for payload verification.

pricing.paid_tiers: Starter $39/month (1,000 pages); Professional $99/month; Business $249/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: German company (Regensburg). Founded 2014. Bootstrapped. Simple rule-based parsing with visual template builder. Zonal OCR extraction. Strong SMB and mid-market adoption. Zapier, Make, and n8n integrations. Webhook for real-time notifications. Page-based pricing model.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for document import, status polling, and result retrieval. API key Basic auth. Good developer documentation. Parser templates must be configured in UI first before API use. Processing is async — upload then poll or use webhook. Results returned as JSON with parser-defined field names.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Document uploads create new records — not idempotent by content. Result reads fully idempotent by document ID. Parser configuration reads idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Parser templates must be configured in Docparser UI — API cannot create templates
Parser ID required for every document upload — different parsers for different document types
Processing is asynchronous — implement webhook or polling for completion
Document page limits affect throughput — plan capacity accordingly
API key in Basic auth: use key as username with empty password — not a Bearer token

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. EU and US data residency. API key Basic auth (weaker than Bearer token). Documents may contain sensitive financial/legal data — ensure encryption in transit and at rest. Webhook signature verification recommended. German company with EU data handling standards.
